24	## Appearances & Description
25	
26	> *See also: [Images of Mycah](wiki:Category:Images_of_Mycah)*
27	
28	He is an ugly boy, with a rough face, freckles, and red hair.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)
29	
30	## Narrative Arc
31	
32	### A Game of Thrones
33	
34	Mycah is a butcher's boy who [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) befriends on the ride south from [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). He sleeps in the meat [wagon](wiki:Wagon) and smells accordingly. Just seeing him makes [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) sick. Arya and Mycah start exploring together the lands through which the track passes. Mycah shows Arya a [lizard lion](wiki:Lizard_lion) when they come through the [Neck](wiki:Neck) and, when they reach the [ruby ford](wiki:Ruby_ford), they intend to search the [Trident](wiki:Trident) for rubies from the armor of Prince [Rhaegar Targaryen](wiki:Rhaegar_Targaryen).(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)
35	
36	He and Arya are practicing sword-fighting on the banks of the Trident. Mycah lands a hit on Arya's hand with his stick, just as Prince [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon) and Sansa come upon them. Joffrey, slightly drunk on [wine](wiki:Wine), is amused about the scene and only Sansa shouting at Arya makes him aware that this is her sister. Joffrey asks the boy who he is, but Mycah is too afraid to answer the prince. Sansa says that he's the butcher's boy, but Arya corrects her, introducing Mycah as her friend. Joffrey mocks that a butcher boy wants to be a [knight](wiki:Knight), then suggests to fight Mycah himself, but with his [sword](wiki:Sword) [Lion's Tooth](wiki:Lion's_Tooth) rather than with a stick, asking Mycah whether he only dares to fight girls. Mycah insists that Arya asked him to fight her and Sansa realizes that he's telling the truth. However, Joffrey intimidates Mycah by asking him whether he knew he hurt his betrothed's sister. He lays the point of his sword on Mycah's cheek, drawing some blood. While Mycah is frozen in fear, Arya shouts that Joffrey should stop it, grabs her stick, and too late for Sansa to interfere, hits Joffrey on the head, causing a laceration. While Mycah flees the scene, Joffrey whirls around at Arya in anger and manages to defend himself against a second attack, splintering Arya's stick. Arya hurls a rock at Joffrey, but misses him. Joffrey goes after her, cursing. Before he can reach Arya, he is attacked by her [direwolf](wiki:Direwolf) [Nymeria](wiki:Nymeria) who mangles his sword arm, causing him to drop his sword and to whimper in pain. Arya picks up Lion's Tooth and throws it into the river, then runs away.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)
37	
38	Arya is found after four days and brought before King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) and Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister), who have taken Ser [Raymun Darry](wiki:Raymun_Darry)'s [Castle Darry](wiki:Castle_Darry) as their temporary residence while both banks of the Trident were searched for Arya and Mycah. Cersei accuses the two of having attacked Joffrey together, a version of events that is confirmed by Joffrey. Arya calls Joffrey a liar. They both present their very different stories. Then Sansa is called as witness. Afraid that contradicting Joffrey would put an end to their betrothal, Sansa claims she can't remember as everything went down so fast, arousing Arya's anger. King Robert decides that both Arya and Joffrey should be disciplined, but Queen Cersei insists that Sansa's direwolf [Lady](wiki:Lady_(direwolf)) should be killed, in place for Nymeria who has vanished. As Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) returns after he has put down the direwolf, he comes across [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane), who has returned with a search party, carrying the body of Mycah in a cloak. The blow of the Hound's sword has almost cut the boy in half from shoulder to waist, as Eddard notices in horror.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Ragot16.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)
39	
40	The events cause deep resentment in Arya against Cersei, Joffrey, and the Hound. She believes in [Jeyne Poole](wiki:Jeyne_Poole)'s elaborated version of the story, where Mycah's body was cut in so many pieces that, when the sack containing the remains was given to Mycah's father, the butcher believed he was handed a slaughtered pig. Arya is also angry with Sansa for not telling the truth about what happened and with her father and his men for allowing Mycah to be killed. She also feels guilt over his death, assuming that nothing would have happened to the butcher's boy if she hadn't talked him into practicing sword fighting. When she tells her father about this, Lord Eddard consoles her, explaining that this is solely the guilt of the Hound and of the cruel woman he serves.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Ragot22.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)
41	
42	Following the attack on Lord Eddard by [Lannister men](wiki:House_Lannister_guards) led by Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister), in which several [Stark guards](wiki:House_Stark_guards) are killed, Arya says to Sansa that Jaime should be beheaded for this, as should the Hound for killing Mycah. When Sansa insists that the Hound is Joffrey's [sworn shield](wiki:Sworn_shield) and that Arya and Mycah attacked Joffrey, Arya calls her a liar.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Ragot44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)
43	
44	### A Storm of Swords
45	
46	When [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane) is captured by Lord [Beric Dondarrion](wiki:Beric_Dondarrion) and the [brotherhood without banners](wiki:Brotherhood_without_banners), he is put on trial for the offenses of the [Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) army in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). After he denies the charges, [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) informs them that Sandor is guilty of killing Mycah. While he does not deny killing Mycah, Sandor defends himself, saying that he was told the boy attacked Prince [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon) and as his [sworn shield](wiki:Sworn_shield) he had a right to kill anyone who would harm the prince. Since no one knows the truth of the charge, the Hound is forced to participate in a [trial by combat](wiki:Trial_by_combat). The Hound wins and is set free.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Rasos34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) However, later when he is trying to provoke Arya Stark to kill him, he states that he cut Mycah -- calling him "Michael" -- near in half and laughed about it.(wiki:Mycah.cite_ref-Rasos74.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)
47

29	## Origins
30	
31	Following the [tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_Prince_Joffrey's_name_day), Myrcella and her siblings traveled with [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) to [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock) with their grandfather, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)
32	
33	Myrcella has a garden at the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)
34	
35	## Appearances & Description
36	
37	> *See also: [Images of Myrcella Baratheon](wiki:Category:Images_of_Myrcella_Baratheon)*
38	
39	Myrcella has golden curls,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) [emerald](wiki:Emerald) eyes,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) and full lips.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) She is described by her uncle, [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), as having all of her mother [Cersei's](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) beauty, but none of her mother's nature.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Myrcella is delicate, beautiful, and courteous.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) For her age, she displays courage, a strong will, and high intelligence.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) She is eight,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragotappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) or almost eight,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) at the beginning of *[A Song of Ice and Fire](wiki:A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire)*. Myrcella resembles [Rosamund Lannister](wiki:Rosamund_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)
40	
41	## Narrative Arc
42	
43	### A Game of Thrones
44	
45	Princess Myrcella accompanies her father, King [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_Baratheon), north to visit [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) and the [Starks](wiki:House_Stark). She is smitten with [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) during the feast.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1) She rides in the royal [wheelhouse](wiki:Wheelhouse) with her mother, Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)
46	
47	Myrcella and Prince [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) do not recognize a dirty [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) when she chases cats in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)
48	
49	Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the new [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), discovers that Myrcella and her siblings are not the biological children of Robert, but actually of Cersei's twin brother, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) With Robert dying from being gored by a [boar](wiki:Boar), Lord [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) suggests that Tommen and Myrcella be seized and that Robb wed Myrcella. Eddard refuses, however.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)
50	
51	After Robert's death, Myrcella is in the [throne room](wiki:Great_Hall_(Red_Keep)) when Eddard confronts Cersei and is betrayed by Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish), then [arrested](wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)
52	
53	### A Clash of Kings
54	
55	Myrcella attends the [tourney on King Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_King_Joffrey's_name_day).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0) Announcing his claim to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) declares that his niece and her siblings are abominations born of incest.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)
56	
57	In an effort to win [Dorne](wiki:Dorne) to the side of [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) in the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings), Myrcella's uncle, [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), offers to marry her to [Trystane Martell](wiki:Trystane_Martell), the youngest son of [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell), [Prince of Dorne](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) The offer is accepted and Princess Myrcella, accompanied by her [sworn shield](wiki:Sworn_shield) Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), sets sail on the *[Seaswift](wiki:Seaswift)* for [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), where they will be met by an escort from Dorne. The [riot of King's Landing](wiki:Riot_of_King's_Landing) breaks out after Myrcella leaves the harbor.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)
58

## Identity

Myrcella Baratheon is a character.human from the AWOIAF wiki.

## Edges

- HOLDS_TITLE: Princess (track_b: Title)
- SWORN_TO: House Baratheon of King's Landing (track_b: Allegiance)
- CULTURE_OF: Crownlands (track_b: Culture)

- PARENT_OF (reverse): Jaime Lannister (track_b: Fathers) [biologically]
- PARENT_OF (reverse): Cersei Lannister (track_b: Mother)

## Origins

Following the [tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_Prince_Joffrey's_name_day), Myrcella and her siblings traveled with [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) to [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock) with their grandfather, Lord [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

Myrcella has a garden at the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

## Appearances & Description

> *See also: [Images of Myrcella Baratheon](wiki:Category:Images_of_Myrcella_Baratheon)*

Myrcella has golden curls,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) [emerald](wiki:Emerald) eyes,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) and full lips.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) She is described by her uncle, [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), as having all of her mother [Cersei's](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) beauty, but none of her mother's nature.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0) Myrcella is delicate, beautiful, and courteous.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0) For her age, she displays courage, a strong will, and high intelligence.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-1) She is eight,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragotappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) or almost eight,(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) at the beginning of *[A Song of Ice and Fire](wiki:A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire)*. Myrcella resembles [Rosamund Lannister](wiki:Rosamund_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc21.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones

Princess Myrcella accompanies her father, King [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_Baratheon), north to visit [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) and the [Starks](wiki:House_Stark). She is smitten with [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) during the feast.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot5.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1) She rides in the royal [wheelhouse](wiki:Wheelhouse) with her mother, Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)

Myrcella and Prince [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon) do not recognize a dirty [Arya Stark](wiki:Arya_Stark) when she chases cats in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the new [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King), discovers that Myrcella and her siblings are not the biological children of Robert, but actually of Cersei's twin brother, Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) With Robert dying from being gored by a [boar](wiki:Boar), Lord [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) suggests that Tommen and Myrcella be seized and that Robb wed Myrcella. Eddard refuses, however.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)

After Robert's death, Myrcella is in the [throne room](wiki:Great_Hall_(Red_Keep)) when Eddard confronts Cersei and is betrayed by Lord [Petyr Baelish](wiki:Petyr_Baelish), then [arrested](wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)

### A Clash of Kings

Myrcella attends the [tourney on King Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_King_Joffrey's_name_day).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-0) Announcing his claim to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne), [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) declares that his niece and her siblings are abominations born of incest.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0)

In an effort to win [Dorne](wiki:Dorne) to the side of [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) in the [War of the Five Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Five_Kings), Myrcella's uncle, [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), offers to marry her to [Trystane Martell](wiki:Trystane_Martell), the youngest son of [Doran Martell](wiki:Doran_Martell), [Prince of Dorne](wiki:Prince_of_Dorne).(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) The offer is accepted and Princess Myrcella, accompanied by her [sworn shield](wiki:Sworn_shield) Ser [Arys Oakheart](wiki:Arys_Oakheart) of the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard), sets sail on the *[Seaswift](wiki:Seaswift)* for [Braavos](wiki:Braavos), where they will be met by an escort from Dorne. The [riot of King's Landing](wiki:Riot_of_King's_Landing) breaks out after Myrcella leaves the harbor.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-1)

### A Storm of Swords

Myrcella arrives at [Sunspear](wiki:Sunspear) and befriends Princess [Arianne](wiki:Arianne_Martell) and Prince Trystane, the children of Prince Doran.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Rasos4.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) Her chambers are in the [Old Palace](wiki:Old_Palace)'s Tower of the Sun.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc13.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-2)

Maester [Caleotte](wiki:Caleotte) saves her life, but Myrcella loses an ear and has a hideous facial scar.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Raffc40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0)

### A Dance with Dragons

Tyrion, fled in exile to [Pentos](wiki:Pentos), considers crowning Myrcella, but is convinced by [Illyrio Mopatis](wiki:Illyrio_Mopatis) to support [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) instead.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwd1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0)

Ser [Balon Swann](wiki:Balon_Swann) arrives at Sunspear, where he tells Prince Doran that King Tommen wishes his sister to return to King's Landing for a visit, inviting along Myrcella's betrothed, Prince Trystane. Doran assures the knight they will see Myrcella at the Water Gardens.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwd38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0)

During a private conversation with Doran and the [Sand Snakes](wiki:Sand_Snakes), Arianne tells [Obara](wiki:Obara_Sand), [Nymeria](wiki:Nymeria_Sand) and Tyene Sand that Myrcella will tell Balon that her Kingsguard, Arys, had been slain by Gerold Dayne, and that Gerold had tried to kill Myrcella as well. Prince Doran is convinced Myrcella will ask Balon to hunt down Gerold, and that Balon will not be able to refuse. Meanwhile, he decides that Myrcella will, indeed, return to King's Landing, in the company of Nym.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwd38.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-1)

Reflecting in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands), Jaime Lannister thinks he will need to tell Myrcella the truth about her paternity, though the Dornishmen will not like it.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwd48.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_26-0)

During her imprisonment by the [Faith](wiki:Faith_of_the_Seven), Cersei Lannister learns of Gerold's attack on her daughter from her uncle, Ser [Kevan Lannister](wiki:Kevan_Lannister). Kevan tells her that Myrcella blames Darkstar for both the attack on her, as well as the death of Arys.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwd54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_27-0) During a meeting of the [small council](wiki:Small_council), Kevan discusses the preparations to be made for Myrcella's return. The princess has begun her journey to King's Landing with Nymeria.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Radwdepilogue.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_28-0)

> **Joffrey**: Don't be childish. **Myrcella**: We're children. We're **supposed** to be childish.(wiki:Myrcella_Baratheon.cite_ref-Racok2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_17-1)
